chapter_title.en: Chapter 80 – The Bartering regarding the foodstuff

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/195)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/195)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْحِنْطَةِ وَ الشَّعِيرِ فَقَالَ إِذَا كَانَا سَوَاءً فَلَا بَأْسَ
قَالَ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْحِنْطَةِ وَ الدَّقِيقِ فَقَالَ إِذَا كَانَا سَوَاءً فَلَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Usman Bin Isa, from Sama’at who said,
‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the wheat and the barley, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If they are equated, so there is no problem’.
He (the narrator) said, ‘And I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the wheat and the flour, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When they are equated, so there is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Sama‘ah who has said the following: “I once asked him (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about wheat and barley. He (the Imam) said, ‘If they are of equal measures it is not harmful.’ He (the narrator) has said, ‘I asked him (the Imam) about flour and wheat. He (the Imam) said, ‘If they are of equal measures it is not harmful.’”
